definition: Vol-targeting and risk-parity strategies ($500B-$1T AUM) are a structural amplifier of correlation regime transitions with no historical precedent. Risk parity levers bonds to equalize risk contribution, adding risk on both sides under positive correlation. The basis trade ($800B-$1T+ notional at 50-100x leverage) amplifies forced selling. No historical transition (1967, 1994, 1998) included these strategies at scale.
evidence: Risk parity portfolios lever bonds to equalize risk contribution — positive correlation adds risk on both sides
Vol-targeting strategies mechanically reduce exposure when portfolio vol rises from correlation shift
Basis trade at $800B-$1T+ notional at 50-100x leverage amplifies forced selling in Treasuries
No historical correlation regime transition included these strategies at scale — precedent-free
open_questions: Will transitions actually be faster/more volatile than historical episodes, or will the strategies adapt?
How does the basis trade unwind interact with correlation regime shifts?
Has any risk parity fund already restructured for positive correlation assumptions?
